content: pgvector applies WHERE filters AFTER HNSW index scan (post-filter): with default hnsw.ef_search=40 and a 10% selective filter only ~4 rows survive, silently degrading recall. pgvector 0.8.0 adds iterative index scans (hnsw.iterative_scan=strict_order|relaxed_order, hnsw.max_scan_tuples cap) which keep pulling candidates until enough rows pass the filter - up to 100x completeness improvement. For very selective filters (small allowed-doc set) exact scan on the filtered subset (no index, or partial index) beats ANN. Best practice: pre-compute allowed-ID set, use iterative scan + WHERE on ACL join, verify with EXPLAIN; never post-filter in application code after retrieval (leakage risk).
